title: Siege of 1941-44
strengths: High-value local reference source for the siege timeline, trapped population, evacuation scale, and famine conditions.
limitations: Not a direct-death-only source; designed as a general historical entry.
notes: States that 2,887,000 people found themselves besieged in September 1941, that up to 1,000,000 were evacuated from 1941 to 1943, and that famine began in late 1941.

citation_short: Saint Petersburg encyclopaedia, Artillery Bombardments of 1941-44
title: Artillery Bombardments of 1941-44
strengths: Direct local-source statement that at least 25,000 civilians were killed by artillery bombardment.
limitations: Provides a lower-bound total rather than a full uncertainty interval; no military death split.
notes: Also states that about 150,000 shells were fired and that the city was exposed on 611 days.

title: Bombings of 1941-44
strengths: Good local source for the scale of air raids and bomb damage in Leningrad.
limitations: Does not publish a general fatality total from air raids.
notes: States that the Luftwaffe launched 364 raids, with about 3,000 aircraft dropping 5,000 high-explosive bombs and about 70,000 fire-bombs.

title: Germans bombard Leningrad | September 19, 1941
strengths: Provides a specific fatality statement for the 19 September raid.
limitations: Secondary source and phrased as 'more than 1,000'; no formal upper bound.
notes: Useful for a single-day air-raid direct-death floor.
